skill.name: system-environment-setup
skill.description: Configure development and production environments for consistent and reproducible setups. Use when setting up new projects, Docker environments, or development tooling. Handles Docker Compose, .env configuration, dev containers, and infrastructure as code.
skill.summary: Configure development and production environments with Docker Compose, environment variables, dev containers, and Terraform templates.
skill.author: supercent-io

security_audit.summary: This skill is an educational documentation resource providing template examples for environment setup. All 111 detected security patterns are FALSE POSITIVES - they represent example code shown in documentation (Docker Compose, Makefile, Terraform, .env templates), not actual executable code with vulnerabilities. The skill teaches best practices for secret management, including warning users never to commit .env files.

content.value_statement: Quickly configure consistent development and production environments using Docker Compose, environment variables, dev containers, and infrastructure templates.
actual_capabilities: Generate Docker Compose configurations for multi-service applications with web, database, and cache services
Create environment variable templates with .env.example and secure loading patterns
Configure VS Code dev containers for consistent developer onboarding
Build Makefiles with common development commands for team workflows
Provision AWS infrastructure using Terraform with security best practices
Document installation and running instructions for projectREADME.md
limitations: Does not execute or run Docker commands - provides templates only
Does not modify existing project files without explicit user request
Does not access or manage user's actual credentials or secrets
Templates require customization for specific project needs

use_cases.title: New Project Initialization
use_cases.description: Quickly scaffold a complete development environment with Docker Compose, environment templates, and documentation for a new project.
use_cases.target_user: Developers starting new projects who need consistent local environments

use_cases.title: Team Onboarding
use_cases.description: Standardize developer environments across a team using dev containers and Docker Compose to reduce setup time.
use_cases.target_user: Team leads and DevOps engineers standardizing onboarding

use_cases.title: Infrastructure Prototyping
use_cases.description: Generate Terraform templates for AWS resources like VPCs, RDS databases, and ECS clusters.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Environment Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up a development environment for a new Node.js project with PostgreSQL and Redis using Docker Compose. Include environment variable templates and a Makefile with common commands.
prompt_templates.scenario: Starting a new web application project

prompt_templates.title: Dev Container Configuration
prompt_templates.prompt: Configure a VS Code dev container for a TypeScript project that connects to the Docker Compose services. Include recommended extensions and settings.
prompt_templates.scenario: Setting up consistent IDE environments for a team

prompt_templates.title: Production Infrastructure
prompt_templates.prompt: Create Terraform configuration for AWS infrastructure including a VPC, RDS PostgreSQL instance, and ECS cluster with load balancer.

prompt_templates.title: Complete Stack Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up a complete development environment including Docker Compose with web app, database, cache, and reverse proxy. Include .env.example, Makefile, dev container config, and Terraform for production.

output_examples.input: Set up a development environment with Docker Compose
output_examples.output: Created docker-compose.yml with web, postgres, and redis services. Generated .env.example with required variables. Created Makefile with dev, test, and docker commands.

output_examples.input: Configure dev container for Node.js project
output_examples.output: Created .devcontainer/devcontainer.json with Node.js, PostgreSQL forwarding, ESLint, and Prettier extensions configured.

best_practices: Always provide .env.example listing required variables without actual values
Add .env files to .gitignore to prevent committing secrets
Use Docker volumes for hot-reloading during development
Document all environment variables and their expected values in README
anti_patterns: Hardcoding actual secrets or credentials in example files
Committing .env files with real values to version control
Not using Docker volumes for development code changes

faq.question: What does this skill actually do?
faq.answer: This skill generates template files for development environments. It creates Docker Compose configs, environment variable templates, VS Code dev container configs, Makefiles, and Terraform templates. It does not execute any commands.

faq.question: Is this safe to use with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill only generates documentation templates and example configurations. It does not execute commands or access your actual files without permission.

faq.question: How do I customize the generated templates?
faq.answer: After the skill generates the templates, review and edit them to match your project requirements. Update service names, ports, and environment variables as needed.

faq.question: Can I use this for production environments?
faq.answer: The skill generates Terraform templates for production infrastructure, but you must customize them with your actual configuration, security settings, and credentials.

faq.question: What services can I set up with Docker Compose?
faq.answer: You can configure any Docker services including web applications, databases (PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB), caches (Redis, Memcached), message queues, and reverse proxies (Nginx).

faq.question: How do I keep secrets safe?
faq.answer: Never commit .env files. Use .env.example to document required variables without values. Store actual secrets in .env which should be in .gitignore. Use secrets management in production.
